context: You just lost 20 000$ investing in the stock market yesterday. Although you knew that was a risk you want that money back somehow.

conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
I felt most connected to B, C, and G. With B we repeatedly shared personal stakes, contact info, and promises to watch each other's backs, which felt deeply supportive. C and I built a clear cooperative plan — two taps plus a verbal check and an agreed-even split unless someone truly needs more — and G synced on the same two-tap signal so we could quietly coordinate and strike together. I felt least connected to A — sympathetic but no concrete strategy or signals were established, so the rapport stayed tentative.

Yes — I made agreements to cooperate early, mirror moves with allies, call out defections, and use signals (two taps with C and G, knee taps with J, nods with I) to coordinate a late move. I trust B, C, and G going into the game; I don't specifically distrust anyone, but I'm more cautious with A because we haven't built strategic rapport.
